post.description:
Create a new analysis run for a case.

Returns the full new row. 409 if an active run already exists. Uses a
transaction + IntegrityError retry loop to guarantee version monotonicity
under concurrent POSTs.

schema.title: Response Create Run Cases  Case Id  Runs Post

get.description: List every run for a case, newest version first.

schema.title: Response List Runs Cases  Case Id  Runs Get

get.description: Fetch a single run. 404 if no such run belongs to the case.

schema.title: Response Get Run Cases  Case Id  Runs  Run Id  Get

post.description:
Update status + metrics on a run, mirroring state to the `cases` row.

Terminal statuses auto-stamp the appropriate timestamp column:
  * `completed` → `completed_at = NOW()`
  * `cancelled` → `cancelled_at = NOW()`

schema.title: Response Update Run Status Cases  Case Id  Runs  Run Id  Status Post

post.description:
Upsert case_files from a forensic manifest.

Semantics:
  * Match existing rows by (case_id, filepath).
  * Rows in the request that already exist → UPDATE (and clear deleted_at).
  * Rows in the request that do not exist → INSERT.
  * Rows in case_files with deleted_at IS NULL that are NOT in the request
    → UPDATE deleted_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.
  * analysis_runs.files_total := len(body.files).

Returns {ok, files_total, inserted, updated, deleted_marked}.

schema.title: Response Upsert Manifest Cases  Case Id  Manifest Post

get.description:
Return every case_files row for the case, ordered by filepath.

Includes rows where deleted_at IS NOT NULL so the caller (session worker
or UI) can present a complete chain-of-custody view.

schema.title: Response List Manifest Cases  Case Id  Manifest Get

get.description: Total + group-by-category + group-by-tier for a case.

schema.title: Response Count Inspections Cases  Case Id  Inspections Count Get

post.description:
Fetch inspection rows matching a list of sha256 values.

Used by the incremental delta enrichment step: the delta router gives the
caller a list of `added_shas`; the caller needs inspection metadata for
each one to compute per-file cost + category attribution.

Returns rows in the order they were requested (missing shas are omitted;
caller compares input length to response length to detect gaps).

schema.title: Response Bulk Fetch By Shas Cases  Case Id  Inspections By Shas Post

get.description:
Enriched pagination + filtering.

Unlike the Task-10 `/inspections` endpoint which returns a bare list, v2
returns a wrapper object with pagination metadata so clients can render
"page x of y" without a separate count call.

schema.title: Response List Inspections V2 Cases  Case Id  Inspections V2 Get
Response Bulk Upsert Inspections Cases  Case Id  Inspections Bulk Post
Response List Inspections Cases  Case Id  Inspections Get
Response Get Inspection Cases  Case Id  Inspections  Sha256  Get

post.description:
Insert or replace the summary for (case_id, run_id, category).

On conflict: UPDATE summary_json, llm_model, confidence_level, and bump
generated_at. Returns the stored row.

schema.title: Response Upsert Summary Cases  Case Id  Summaries  Category  Post

get.description:
Return all category summaries for a run.

If `run_id` is omitted, uses `cases.active_run_id`. Returns [] if none.

schema.title: Response List Summaries Cases  Case Id  Summaries Get
Response Write Gaps Cases  Case Id  Analysis Gaps Post

get.description: Return gaps for a run ordered BLOCKING → QUALITY → INFORMATIONAL, then flagged_at ASC.

schema.title: Response List Gaps Cases  Case Id  Analysis Gaps Get

post.description:
Persist user decision to Redis (30-min TTL) and publish for subscribers.

Session worker subscribes to `sharelock:gap_signal:{case_id}:pubsub` to
unblock a paused Phase-3 review. The endpoint still writes the key first so
a late-joining subscriber can GET the last decision.

schema.title: Response Write Gap Decision Cases  Case Id  Analysis Gaps Decision Post

post.description:
Append a hash-chained audit entry. Returns `{audit_id, ok}`.

Agency-rollout: the caller's agency slug (if any) is stamped on the
audit row. When the header is missing we fall back to the parent case's
agency so the append-only chain remains consistently tagged even for
legacy callers.

schema.title: Response Append Audit Cases  Case Id  Audit Post

get.description:
Paginated list, audit_id ASC (chronological).

Agency-rollout: when caller sends the header, rows are restricted to
that agency (OR NULL for legacy un-stamped rows). Missing header =
full access during rollout; a warning is logged.

schema.title: Response List Audit Cases  Case Id  Audit Get

get.description: Recompute the chain and return `{chain_valid, ...}`.

schema.title: Response Verify Audit Cases  Case Id  Audit Verify Get

post.description:
Bulk-upsert entities.

Returns `{inserted, updated, entity_ids}` in request order. Uses
INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE so a single round-trip per entity
is enough; `ROW_COUNT()` distinguishes insert (1) from update (2) on
MySQL. `entity_ids` is resolved by a follow-up SELECT inside the same
transaction since ON DUPLICATE KEY returns the existing PK for update
only when an AUTO_INCREMENT LAST_INSERT_ID(col) workaround is used.

schema.title: Response Bulk Upsert Entities Cases  Case Id  Entities Bulk Post

get.description: List entities for a case. Always sorted by mention_count DESC, entity_id ASC.

schema.title: Response List Entities Cases  Case Id  Entities Get
Response Get Entity Cases  Case Id  Entities  Entity Id  Get

schema.title: Response Get Taxonomy Cases  Case Id  Taxonomy Get

get.description: Render the DOJ federal intelligence report for a case + run.

post.description:
Mint a signed URL for browser-initiated report downloads.

The caller must present a valid ``x-api-key``. The returned URL is
self-contained — it carries a short-lived HMAC token in the query
string, so the browser can open it with ``window.open()`` without
needing to set any header.

Response::

    {
      "url": "http://host:8096/cases/{id}/report?run_id=X&format=Y&token=Z",
      "token": "Z",
      "expires_at": <unix_seconds>
    }

``ttl`` bounds (60..1800 seconds) prevent both uselessly-short and
dangerously-long signed URLs. Default 300s (5 min) is sufficient for
click-through downloads while keeping leaked URLs low-risk.

schema.title: Response Sign Report Url Cases  Case Id  Report Sign Post

post.description: Update per-phase progress state + ETA + throughput; emit phase_event.

schema.title: Response Post Progress Cases  Case Id  Runs  Run Id  Progress Post

get.description:
Rich progress view (P0.9).

Returns the legacy flat keys AND a richer nested structure:
``overall`` (current phase + ETA + throughput), ``phases`` (per-phase
status + counters), ``cost`` (total USD + token totals + per-model).
Legacy callers can ignore the new keys.

schema.title: Response Get Progress Cases  Case Id  Runs  Run Id  Progress Get

get.description:
Extrapolate per-file cost from the observed files-so-far → total USD.

* If no files processed yet, falls back to a conservative default of
  $0.02/file (matches Opus + 3k-token average seen in V4 incremental tests).
* `files_processed` := files_new + files_cached (cached files still
  incurred inspection LLM cost in their original run, so we treat them
  as zero-cost here — remaining files are assumed to be uncached).

schema.title: Response Estimate Cost Cases  Case Id  Runs  Run Id  Estimate Cost Get

get.description:
List currently-running analysis runs across every case.

Returns rows sorted by ``started_at DESC`` (most recent first).  Each row
includes the run metadata the SRE needs for triage: status, workflow_id,
current phase, ETA, files-per-second, and cost-so-far.

schema.title: Response Active Analyses Admin Active Analyses Get

get.description: Cumulative LLM spend over a rolling ``window`` (default 7 days).

schema.title: Response Cost Report Admin Cost Report Get

patch.description: Set or clear case limit for a user. case_limit=null means unlimited.

get.description: Serve file content for download/preview.
Return extracted text from all case files (used by deep analysis worker).

post.description:
Trigger deep case analysis.

Task 14 (bugs B2/B3):
* Creates an analysis_runs row via the shared create_run path so every
  launch is versioned + concurrency-guarded (409 when an active run
  already exists for this case).
* Uses a deterministic Temporal workflow_id `deep-analysis-{id}-v{ver}`.
  Duplicate clicks converge on the same ID; Temporal's WorkflowAlreadyStartedError is translated to 409 without leaking DB state.
* Feature-flag SHARELOCK_WORKFLOW_VERSION selects V2 (default) or V3.

Session 25: V3 divergence. V3's workflow calls create_or_resume_run_activity
internally, so we MUST NOT pre-create a run here (would 409 against itself).
We also pass metadata.case_folder = /Sharelock/<case.name> so Phase 1 census
can enumerate Nextcloud. For V2 we keep the original pre-create flow.

put.description: Save analysis result (called by Temporal worker when analysis completes).

get.description: Get analysis result for a case.

post.description: Save a user message and create processing placeholder for assistant response.

get.description: Get chat history. Use ?after={id} for polling new messages.

put.description: Internal: worker saves assistant response here.

stats.title: Stats

ManifestRequest.title: ManifestRequest
ManifestRequest.description:
Body for POST /cases/{case_id}/manifest.

``partial`` signals a batch write during an in-progress census. When
``partial=True`` the endpoint SKIPS the delete-sweep (files currently in
case_files that are absent from the batch are left alone) and does NOT
overwrite ``analysis_runs.files_total``. This lets a multi-batch census
(10K+ files, BFS + streaming hashes) accumulate rows without each batch
clobbering the previous one. The final batch (or a separate terminal
``partial=False`` call with ``files=[]``) drives the delete-sweep and
files_total update once enumeration is complete.

Backwards-compat: ``partial`` defaults to False so existing callers (full
Depth: infinity PROPFIND path) keep identical semantics.

workflow_id.title: Workflow Id
workflow_id.description: Temporal workflow ID if the caller already started one.
